Skip to the content.

Install via Homebrew

Install Scratchpad via the aaronmyatt/scratchpad tap.

Status: Cask formula is written and ready; tap repo goes live alongside the first GitHub Release (v0.1.0). Until then, use curl | bash or the direct download.

One-liner

brew install aaronmyatt/scratchpad/scratchpad

Or explicitly:

brew tap aaronmyatt/scratchpad
brew install scratchpad

What this does

  1. Adds the aaronmyatt/scratchpad tap (a third-party Homebrew repo).
  2. Downloads the latest Scratchpad-arm64.tar.gz release artifact from github.com/aaronmyatt/scratchpad/releases.
  3. Verifies the sha256 against the value pinned in the Cask formula.
  4. Extracts Scratchpad.app into /Applications.
  5. Strips the com.apple.quarantine extended attribute as part of brew’s standard install flow — so the app launches with no Gatekeeper prompt.

Updating

brew update
brew upgrade scratchpad

Uninstalling

brew uninstall scratchpad
brew untap aaronmyatt/scratchpad   # optional

After install

On first launch, Scratchpad offers to install the small sp CLI on your PATH so you can pipe text from any terminal:

echo "hello" | sp

Accept the prompt — by default it installs to /usr/local/bin/sp (or ~/bin/sp if /usr/local/bin isn’t writable, with PATH guidance).

Why brew is the friction-free path

Homebrew downloads release artifacts via curl (not via a browser), so the com.apple.quarantine extended attribute that triggers Gatekeeper never gets attached. Plus, brew explicitly strips the attribute on install as belt-and-braces.

The direct DMG download path hits Gatekeeper because browsers do attach the attribute — workarounds are documented there for users who prefer the visual install experience.

See decision-3 for the full distribution-strategy rationale (why Scratchpad isn’t notarized for v1 and why brew/curl bypass that gracefully).

This page was generated by GitHub Pages.
